GEORGE E. PARAS (RETIRED)

OF COUNSEL
IRVING S. BERLUSS
PHILIP M. KNOX, JR.
MATTHEW C. MARTIN

GERALD R. JOHNSON (1857-1973)

1750 MONTGOMERY STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94111
(415) 543-9564

870 MANZANITA COURT
POST OFFICE BOX 3590
CHICO, CALIFORNIA 95927-3590
(916) 893-0000

January 16, 1990

Mr. Jeevan Ahuja
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:  Use of Campaign Funds for Travel Reimbursement
     for Members of the California-Nevada Super Speed
     Ground Transportation Commission Who are Also
     Elected Officials

Dear Mr. Ahuja:

     I am glad that we were finally able to speak to one another.

     This letter will serve to formalize the request I made
orally on the telephone the other day for an opinion from your
offices regarding whether commissioners of the California-Nevada
Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission ("the Commission")
who are also California elected officials, may use campaign funds
for travel planned by the Commission including a trip to Germany
to inspect and evaluate a Super Speed Train.  During the trip,
the Commissioners will also speak with representatives of the
Bechtel Corporation to discuss Bechtel's interest in owning the
franchise to construct, maintain and operate the Super Speed
Train proposed by the Commission.  For your review, I enclose a
copy of the California legislation creating the Commission.

     According to Government Code Sections 85802 and 85802(a)(1),
we understand that reimbursement from campaign funds for such
traveling would be permissible so long as such reimbursements
meet the standards of the Internal Revenue Service contained in
Sections 162 and 274 of the Internal Revenue Code for deductions
of travel expenses under Federal Income Tax Law.

GREVE, CLIFFORD, DIEPENBROCK & PARAS

Mr. Jeevan Ahuja
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 16, 1990
Page 2


     Thank you for your consideration of our request.  Please
keep in mind that the trip to Germany is to occur during the week
March 12 through 16, 1990, and the commissioners require adequate
lead time to determine whether such a trip is feasible.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Peter B. Lemmon

PDL/rlk

Enclosure

cc:  Paul Taylor

\calnev\fppc.lt

§ 92351

GOVERNMENT CODE

Rapid rail transit system facilities and projects, as defined in Section 92015, may be acquired,
constructed, completed, repaired, altered, improved, or extended, and bonds may be issued for any of
these purposes under this title, notwithstanding that any other law may provide for the acquisition,
construction, completion, repair, alteration, improvement, or extension of similar facilities or for the
issuance of bonds for similar purposes and without regard to the requirements, restrictions, limitations,
or other provisions contained in any other law.
Added Stats 1982 ch 1553 § 2.

§ 92352. Limitations or alterations of rights vested in commission

The State of California hereby pledges to, and agrees with, the holders of any obligations issued under
this title, and with those parties who may enter into contracts with the commission pursuant to this title,
that the state will not limit or alter the rights hereby vested in the commission until the obligations,
together with the interest thereon, are fully met and discharged and the contracts are fully performed on
the part of the commission. However, nothing in this section precludes such limitation or alteration if
and when adequate provision has been made by law for the protection of the holders of obligations of the
commission or those entering into contracts with the commission.

The commission, as agent for the state, may include this pledge and undertaking for the state in its
obligations and contracts.
Added Stats 1982 ch 1553 § 2.

§ 92353. Provisions as controlling

To the extent that the provisions of this title, with respect to the financing of rapid rail systems, are
inconsistent with the provisions of any general statute or special act or parts thereof, with respect to the
financing of rapid rail transit facilities, the provisions of this title shall be deemed controlling.
Added Stats 1982 ch 1553 § 2.

§ 92354. Reimbursement for costs related to rapid rail transit system or project

Any state agency whose support is provided for in the Budget Act shall require any railroad corporation
proposing to construct or operate a rapid rail transit system or project, as defined in Section 92015, to
reimburse the agency for its costs for processing any application or approval related to the system or
project. The corporation shall also reimburse the agency for all costs directly related to any application or
approval including, but not limited to, costs of agency staff, consultants, and any advisory services which
the agency deems necessary.
Added Stats 1983 ch 323 § 5 6, effective July 21, 1983.


§§ 93000-93002. [Title 12, California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Compact] was
added by Stats 1987 ch 1259 § 1 and repealed by State 1988 ch 149 sec 6, effective June 10, 1988.]

§ 93000. [Section repealed 1988.]
Added Stats 1987 ch 1259 § 1. Repealed Stats 1988 ch 149 sec 6, effective June 10, 1988. The repealed section related to
ratification and approval of the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Compact.
Former Section: Former § 93000 was added by Stats 1984 ch 615 § 1, effective July 19, 1984, and repealed, effective
January 1, 1986, by the terms of § 93033.
Note—Stats 1988 ch 149 § 5 provides:

SECTION 1 This act shall be known as the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Act.

SEC. 2 It is found and declared that the passage of this act is a declaration of legislative intent that the State of
California should actively encourage and assist, if justified, pursue the development of a super speed ground transportation
system connecting southern California with southern Nevada.

(b) The system will serve to:
(1) Provide economic benefits to both southern California and southern Nevada
(2) Reduce reliance on gasoline and diesel fueled engines and encourage the use of alternative energy sources.
(3) Reduce congestion on Interstate 15 between southern California and Las Vegas
(4) Provide a working example for a state-of-the-art transportation system that could play an essential role in the
development of future commuter service in the Los Angeles basin and the Las Vegas valley;
(5) Provide quick and convenient transportation service for residents and visitors visiting in southern California and
southern Nevada.

As used in this act:

(a) "Commission" means the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission.
(b) "Southern California" means the Counties of Los Angeles, Orange, Riverside, and San Bernardino.
(c) "Super Speed Ground Transportation System" means a system that is capable of speeds of at least 180 miles-per-
hour and primarily serves passengers and operates on a grade separated, dedicated guideway.

SEC. 3 (a) There is hereby created the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission.

(b) The governing body of the commission shall be constituted as follows:

(1) California delegation:
(A) Four members appointed by the Governor of California who shall be residents of southern California, at least
one of whom shall be a resident of the County of Orange.
(B) Two members appointed by the Senate Committee on Rules, one of whom shall be a resident of the County of San
Bernardino and one of whom shall be a resident of the County of Riverside.
(C) Two members appointed by the Speaker of the Assembly, at least one of whom shall be a resident of the County of
Los Angeles.
(2) Nevada delegation of eight members appointed as required by Nevada law.
(3) The commission shall elect one of its members to be the chairperson.
(4) All commission members shall be appointed before June 1, 1988.

268                                    [16 Gov Code]

EXHIBIT "E"          (Page 1 of 2)

GOVERNMENT CODE                                                § 95000

SEC. 5. (a) The commission may prepare a plan for the construction and operation of a
transportation system at no expense to the State of California principally following the route of Interstate Highway 15
between the City of Las Vegas, Nevada, and a point in southern California. The commission shall not include in the
plan a southern California terminus unless the board of supervisors of the county of the proposed site after public
hearings approves that site selection.
   (b) The plan shall be submitted to, and ratified by, both the California
Legislature or Nevada Legislative Commission, as required by Nevada law, before the commission or a franchisee of the
commission begins construction and obtains certificates and permits necessary for construction or use of public right-of-
way.
   (c) Subject to subdivisions (a) and (b), the commission may do any of the following:
   (1) Conduct engineering and other studies related to the technology and the selection of a
franchise, including, but not limited to, environmental impact studies, socioeconomic impact studies, and financial
feasibility studies. All local, state, and federal environmental requirements shall be met by the commission.
   (2) Evaluate alternative super speed rail technologies, systems, and operators, and select a franchisee to build and
operate the super speed rail system between southern California and Las Vegas.
   (3) Award grants, gifts, and allocations for the award of a franchise.
   (4) Accept grants, gifts, and allocations from Nevada or its political subdivisions, the federal government, foreign
governments, and private sources. There shall be no public cost to the State of California or any of its political
subdivisions.
   (5) Issue debt, but this debt shall not constitute an obligation of the States of California or Nevada, or any of their
political subdivisions.
   (6) Hire an executive officer, other staff, and any consultant deemed appropriate.
   (7) Designate a route, terminus, a proposed route, and proposed terminal sites.
   (d) The commission shall incorporate under the nonprofit corporation laws of California. It shall have all the powers
and duties of a nonprofit corporation.
   (e) All meetings of the commission shall be open to the public to the extent required by the law of the State of
California or the State of Nevada, whichever imposes the greater requirement applicable to local governments at the
time meetings are held.
   (f) All members of the commission who are California-elected officials shall be subject to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act of 1974 (Part 9 (commencing with Section 81000) of Division 10 of the Government Code).

SEC. 1. Sections 1 to 5 of this act shall remain in effect only until January 1, 1992, and as of that date are repealed,
unless a later enacted statute, which is enacted before January 1, 1992, deletes or extends that date.

§ 93001. [Section repealed 1988.]
Added Stats 1987 ch 1259 § 1. Repealed Stats 1988 ch 149 sec 6, effective June 10, 1988. The repealed section related to
provisions of the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Compact.
Former Section: Former § 93001 was added by Stats 1984 ch 615 § 1, effective July 19, 1984, and repealed, effective
January 1, 1986, by the terms of § 93033.

§ 93002. [Section repealed 1988.]
Added Stats 1987 ch 1259 § 1. Repealed Stats 1988 ch 149 sec 6, effective June 10, 1988. The repealed section related to
repeal of the title on January 1, 1992.
Former Section: Former § 93002 was added by Stats 1984 ch 615 § 1, effective July 19, 1984, and repealed, effective
January 1, 1986, by the terms of § 93033.

§§ 93010-93034. [Added by Stats 1984 ch 615 § 1, effective July 19, 1984, and repealed, effective
January 1, 1986; by the terms of § 93033.]

TITLE 14
CALIFORNIA EARLY INTERVENTION SERVICES ACT
[Added by Stats 1985 ch 26 § 1, effective April 9, 1985.]

Chapter  1.  General Provisions and Definitions. §§ 95000-95014
         2.  Interagency Task Force on Early Intervention Services. §§ 95030-95037 [Repealed]
         3.  Interagency Demonstration Projects. §§ 95039-95046 [Repealed]

CHAPTER 1
General Provisions and Definitions

Article 1.   General1 Provisions. §§ 95000-95006
         2.   Definitions. §§ 95010-95014 [Repealed]

ARTICLE 1
General Provisions

§ 95000. Citation of title
§ 95001. Legislative findings
§ 95002. Legislative intent regarding existing providers
§ 95003. Legislative intent that state agencies coordinate services
§§ 95004-95006. [Repealed]

§ 95000. Citation of title

This title may be cited as the California Early Intervention Services Act.

[18 Gov Code]                                                                    209

EXHIBIT "E"        (Page 2 of 2)
